text: Block periodization (concentrated HIT blocks every fourth week) produces greater VO2max improvements than traditional evenly-distributed HIT training over 12 weeks in trained cyclists
Block periodization shows a trend toward greater improvement in lactate threshold power output compared to traditional periodization

Block periodization produces moderate superior effect sizes across multiple endurance indices (VO2max, lactate threshold power, hemoglobin mass, 40-min time trial power) compared to traditional training distribution

A block periodization structure of 5 HIT sessions in one concentrated week followed by 3 weeks of 1 HIT session per week is more effective than a steady 2 HIT sessions per week, even when total HIT and LIT volumes are equated
specific_value: 5 HIT/week for 1 week then 1 HIT/week for 3 weeks vs 2 HIT/week steady

text: Concentrating high-intensity training into focused blocks rather than distributing it evenly can improve hemoglobin mass and 40-min all-out performance in already well-trained endurance athletes
